question: How do you declare a relation endpoint in `charmcraft.yaml` and what fields does it support?

claude_output.result:
Relation endpoints are declared in `charmcraft.yaml` under three top-level keys:

```yaml
provides:
  <endpoint-name>:
    interface: <interface-name>
    limit: <int>
    optional: <bool>
    scope: global | container

requires:
  <endpoint-name>:
    interface: <interface-name>
    optional: <bool>

peers:
  <endpoint-name>:
    interface: <interface-name>
```

## Fields

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `interface` | Yes | The interface protocol this endpoint speaks (e.g. `http`, `pgsql`) |
| `optional` | No | Whether the relation is optional (default: `false`). Per the project guidelines, **always include this key** |
| `limit` | No | Max number of connections allowed to this endpoint |
| `scope` | No | `global` (default) or `container` (K8s only — shares data only with the sidecar in the same pod) |

## Endpoint types

- **`provides`** — this charm offers a service to others
- **`requires`** — this charm depends on a service from another charm
- **`peers`** — relations between units of the same application (used for clustering/replication)

## Example

```yaml
provides:
  website:
    interface: http
    optional: false

requires:
  database:
    interface: pgsql
    optional: true
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: charm-gossip
    optional: false
```

The `interface` value is a contract name — both charms on either side of a relation must declare the same interface for the relation to be valid. Canonical maintains a registry of standard interfaces at [charm-relation-interfaces](https://github.com/canonical/charm-relation-interfaces).
